stemHtml: You are the Pilot Flying (PF) and just about to approach your destination. The autopilot is engaged, and as you reduce speed in accordance with your clearance you instinctively trim up significantly on the pitch trim wheel, reverting to the aircraft type you trained on, which had NO auto-trim. The consequence may be…

options.a: That the autopilot may be unable to counteract your trim input quickly enough. The aircraft may pitch down, requiring you to disconnect the autopilot and fly manually.
options.b: That the moment you move the trim wheel the autopilot will disconnect. The aircraft will be perfectly in trim, and you will be able to re-engage the autopilot WITHOUT re-trimming.
options.c: An out-of-trim condition, which may cause the autopilot to disconnect and the aircraft to pitch up.
options.d: That the autopilot will fly the rest of the approach with an out-of-trim load, which may leave insufficient authority should a go-around be necessary. This is corrected by disengaging and re-engaging the autopilot.
